families.displayName: Microplastics and nanoplastics in food and dietary exposure
notes: This family is structurally separate from pesticide-residue workflows even though it is exposed through the same MCP governance surface.
Internal-review and consultation-oriented support are allowed, but submission-oriented use hard-fails by default until a reviewed official method pack changes the posture.
No PRIMo, DEEM, or OpenFoodTox method record is reused as if it were microplastics-native.

families.displayName: PFAS food contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist
notes: This family is exposed as a governed PFAS food-contaminant watchlist with explicit legal, reference-value, and monitoring provenance.
Dietary MCP does not publish a native PFAS submission engine in v0.1 even though official EFSA and EU contaminant sources are represented here.
Submission-oriented use remains blocked through the family-specific submission profile until a reviewed PFAS method pack is added.
Evidence linkage remains transitional and food-context coverage should be reviewed explicitly for fish, eggs, offal, and other higher-attention matrices.

families.displayName: Acrylamide process contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist
notes: This family is exposed as a governed acrylamide process contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist with explicit EFSA and EU legal provenance.
Submission-oriented use is allowed when the dossier meets standard governance and fingerprint requirements.
